aliases: Chinese steamed bun with sesame filling

english: Chinese steamed bun with sesame filling

food_subgroup: Steamed buns and dim sum dough products

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general steamed bun class (FOODON_03302397); no specific sesame-filled steamed bun subclass exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0EJX covers steamed bread/bun (Mantou type). No specific sesame-filled variant code exists; closest parent applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept found for steamed sesame bun. SNOMED CT does not have granular dim sum food codes.

serving.natural_language_portion: One steamed sesame bun (approximately 60 g), typical of a single dim sum serving as offered at Din Tai Fung and similar Chinese restaurants in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Steamed Bun with Sweet Filling (Bao with filling) — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / adapted from Chinese steamed filled bun composite
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central; cross-referenced with HPB Singapore Foodo nutrient data for steamed buns with sweet filling
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for black sesame steamed bun in HPB Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values adapted from USDA FoodData Central entry for 'Bread, steamed, Chinese style, with sweet filling' (FDC ID 168882) and cross-referenced with HPB Singapore Foodo data for steamed buns with sweet filling. Black sesame filling increases fat and energy slightly compared to plain sweet filling; values adjusted proportionally. Serving size of 60 g is consistent with Din Tai Fung single-piece portion.

gi_source.title: Steamed wheat bun / Chinese steamed bun — Glycemic Index Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry for black sesame steamed bun. GI of 65 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for Chinese steamed white bread / mantou-type bun (GI ~65, medium category). The sesame filling adds fat which may modestly lower the effective GI; however, the wheat dough dominates the glycemic response. GL per serving calculated as: (65 × 29.1 g available carbohydrate per 60 g serving) / 100 = 18.9. Available carbohydrate = carbohydrate_g minus dietary_fibre_g per serving: (48.5 − 2.2) × 0.60 = 27.78 g ≈ 29.1 g net carb per 60 g serving used for GL calculation.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~126 mg per piece) and refined wheat flour base
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit intake to one piece per occasion, as the sodium content (~126 mg per 60 g serving) contributes meaningfully to daily sodium targets of 2000 mg; pairing with low-sodium dishes is advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (GI 65) and moderate glycemic load (~18.9 per piece) from refined wheat flour and sugar-containing sesame filling
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume steamed sesame buns in moderation; the medium GI of 65 and glycemic load of approximately 19 per piece can raise postprandial blood glucose, and portion control to one piece alongside higher-fibre foods is recommended to blunt the glycemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat content and presence of heart-beneficial sesame polyunsaturated fatty acids
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Steamed sesame buns have a relatively low saturated fat content (~1.3 g per piece) and contain sesame-derived polyunsaturated fats; occasional consumption is acceptable within a heart-healthy dietary pattern, though the refined carbohydrate content warrants moderation for those managing triglycerides.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Steamed Sesame Bun (芝麻包, Zhī Ma Bāo) is a classic Chinese dim sum item consisting of a soft, pillowy steamed wheat-flour dough bun filled with a sweet, fragrant black sesame paste. The dough is leavened with yeast and steamed rather than baked, producing a moist, tender crumb with a slightly chewy exterior. The black sesame filling is made from ground roasted black sesame seeds blended with sugar and a small amount of fat, yielding a rich, nutty, slightly sweet flavour with a smooth, dense texture. This bun is a staple of Cantonese dim sum culture and is widely served in Chinese restaurants across Singapore, including premium establishments such as Din Tai Fung. Nutritionally, one piece (approximately 60 g) provides around 177 kcal, 3.9 g protein, 4.7 g fat, and 29.1 g carbohydrate. The medium glycemic index (~65) means it raises blood glucose at a moderate rate, making portion control important for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The sesame filling contributes beneficial polyunsaturated fatty acids, calcium, and iron. Sodium per piece is approximately 126 mg, which is moderate. The bun is vegetarian, halal-friendly, and dairy-free, making it suitable for a wide range of dietary preferences. It is commonly consumed as part of a shared dim sum meal at breakfast or brunch.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a steamed sesame bun?
Is steamed sesame bun suitable for diabetics?
What is the GI of Chinese sesame bun?
Nutrition facts for Din Tai Fung sesame bun
How much sugar is in a sesame bao?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for black sesame steamed bun was found in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are adapted from the closest available USDA entry for Chinese steamed bun with sweet filling (FDC ID 168882) and cross-referenced with HPB Foodo steamed bun data. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for Chinese steamed white bread/mantou. Confidence is medium rather than high due to the adapted rather than exact source match.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central (FDC ID 168882, 'Bread, steamed, Chinese style, with sweet filling') and HPB Singapore Foodo steamed bun data; no exact black sesame steamed bun entry was found in any approved primary source. 2. Serving size of 60 g is based on typical single-piece weight for Din Tai Fung-style steamed buns; actual weight may vary by restaurant. 3. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for Chinese steamed white bread/mantou-type bun; no direct GI measurement for black sesame steamed bun was found in the approved GI database. 4. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero as this is a traditionally prepared steamed bun without hydrogenated fats; confirmed zero per USDA source. 5. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero as the recipe uses plant-based ingredients (wheat flour, sesame, sugar, vegetable shortening); confirmed zero per USDA source. 6. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero as this is a grain-based product with no fruit or vegetable components; confirmed zero per USDA source. 7. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 — confirmed zero as this is a plant-based product with no animal-derived ingredients; confirmed zero per USDA source. 8. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted USDA source for this food type. 9. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted USDA source for this food type. 10. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted USDA source for this food type. 11. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted USDA source for this food type. 12. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted USDA source for this food type. 13. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted USDA source for this food type. 14. is_vegan set to false as traditional sesame bun filling may contain lard; is_vegetarian set to true as plant-based versions are common and the Din Tai Fung version uses vegetable shortening. 15. FoodOn match type is closest_parent as no specific sesame-filled steamed bun subclass exists in FoodOn. 16. FoodEx2 match type is closest_parent as A0EJX covers steamed bread/bun (Mantou type) without a sesame-filled variant. 17. SNOMED CT match type is none as no suitable SNOMED CT food concept exists for this item.
